100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 24, 1926, Britain faced a significant political crisis as the General Strike entered its third week, with workers across various sectors demanding better wages and conditions. In the United States, the aviation industry celebrated the successful transatlantic flight by Charles Lindbergh, sparking a surge of interest in air travel. Meanwhile, in Germany, the Weimar Republic struggled with economic instability, as hyperinflation continued to threaten everyday life for its citizens.
Updated: Sunday, May 24, 2026 at 07:51:31 AM CDT
